description: HP 4275A Multi-Frequency LCR Meter with Option 101 HP-IB Compatibility. Measures L, C, R, |Z|, |Y| as the primary (Display A) parameter and D, Q, ESR/G, X/B, L/C, theta as the secondary (Display B) parameter at ten spot test frequencies from 10 kHz to 10 MHz. Remote control uses HP's function-code (letter+number mnemonic) language sent as ASCII strings over HP-IB; the instrument is triggered with the 'E' code. Codes can be concatenated in one string (for example 'A2T3'). With Option 101 the unit can also operate as an addressable or talk-only device, and DC bias (Options 001/002) can be set remotely. Commands are documented in the Operation Manual Section III, Table 3-11 (Remote Program Codes), Table 3-12 (Data Output Codes), and paragraphs 3-66 to 3-95.

commands.name: display_a_function
commands.description: Selects the primary measurement parameter shown on Display A. A1 = L (inductance), A2 = C (capacitance), A3 = R (resistance), A4 = |Z| (impedance magnitude). The valid Display A / Display B combinations are: L-D, L-Q, L-ESR/G; C-D, C-Q, C-ESR/G; R-X/B, R-L/C, R-X/B; |Z|-theta.

commands.name: display_b_function
commands.description: Selects the secondary measurement parameter shown on Display B. B1 = D (dissipation factor), B2 = Q (quality factor), B3 = ESR/G. Combined codes select two-letter secondary parameters: 'B1,B3' = X/B, B2 = L/C, 'B1,B2,B3' = theta. The available secondary parameter depends on the Display A function selected.

parameters.name: code
parameters.range: B1=D, B2=Q, B3=ESR/G, B1,B3=X/B, B2=L/C, B1,B2,B3=theta

commands.name: circuit_mode
commands.description: Selects the equivalent circuit mode used for the measurement. C1 = AUTO, C2 = series, C3 = parallel.

commands.name: deviation_measurement
commands.description: Selects deviation (delta) measurement mode relative to stored reference data. D0 = deviation OFF, D1 = absolute deviation (delta), D2 = percent deviation (delta%). D1 and D2 cannot be used if reference data has not been stored (see Store Reference Value, ST).

commands.name: frequency_step
commands.description: Selects the spot test frequency. F11=10 kHz, F12=20 kHz (30 kHz with Option 004), F13=40 kHz (50 kHz), F14=100 kHz, F15=200 kHz (300 kHz), F16=400 kHz (500 kHz), F17=1 MHz, F18=2 MHz (3 MHz), F19=4 MHz (5 MHz), F20=10 MHz. The frequencies in parentheses are used with Option 004. F21 and F22 are additional frequency steps.

commands.name: high_resolution
commands.description: Sets high-resolution display mode. H0 = OFF, H1 = ON.

commands.name: data_ready
commands.description: Enables or disables the Data Ready service request. I0 = OFF, I1 = ON. When Data Ready is ON, an SRQ signal is generated (status byte bit 1) when measurement data is available.

commands.name: key_state_out
commands.description: Requests output of the current key (setting) status. The instrument returns a string encoding Display A function, Display B function, circuit mode, delta measure, frequency step, high resolution, data ready, multiplier, LCRZ range, self test, and trigger settings. Note: the LCRZ Range is reported as R31 when set to AUTO, so set MANUAL range (R32) to read the true measuring range.

commands.name: level_monitor
commands.description: Requests output of the oscillator test signal level monitored at the unknown terminals. LV = monitor as voltage, LA = monitor as current. The returned data gives the level measurement status, measuring function, and value.

commands.name: multiplier
commands.description: Sets the display multiplier. M1 = x0.01, M2 = x0.1, M3 = x1.

commands.name: lcrz_range
commands.description: Sets the LCRZ measurement range. R31 = AUTO ranging, R32 = MANUAL ranging. R11 through R20 select fixed ranges; the actual L/C/R/Z value of each range depends on the Display A, Display B, and frequency settings (e.g. R11 = 1000 fF / 100 nH, up to R20 = 100 H). If a manually selected range cannot make the measurement, the instrument automatically resets to the nearest range capable of making it.

commands.name: recall_reference_value
commands.description: Recalls and outputs the stored reference data. The data is output as the Display A function followed by its reference value. This code cannot be used if reference data has not been stored.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Controls the instrument self test. S0 = OFF, S1 = ON. Completion of self test sets status byte bit 3; a faulty self test sets status byte bit 4.

commands.name: store_reference_value
commands.description: Stores the current measurement as the reference value, enabling deviation (delta) measurements and the Recall Reference (RE) output.

commands.name: trigger_mode
commands.description: Selects the trigger mode. T1 = INT (internal/continuous), T2 = EXT (external), T3 = HOLD/MANUAL.

commands.name: zero_offset
commands.description: Performs the offset (zero) compensation measurement. ZO = OPEN zero (residual admittance compensation), ZS = SHORT zero (residual impedance compensation). Completion of zero offset sets status byte bit 3.

commands.name: execute_trigger
commands.description: Triggers the instrument to make a measurement from HP-IB and output data. Sent after the measurement-setup program codes.

commands.name: dc_bias
commands.description: Sets the internal DC bias voltage (requires Option 001 or 002). Format is 'BI<mantissa>E<exponent>V' with 3 digits of mantissa and 2 digits of exponent. If polarity of the mantissa or exponent is not specified it defaults to plus (+).
